observed: The repository previously contained hostname derivation and packet validation contracts.
A collector, deterministic tests, and a retained-artifact workflow are now committed.
The available commit workflow-run interface returned no run records for the workflow commit.
A local bounded probe encountered temporary DNS resolver failure and therefore did not establish hostname state.
inferred: Collecting a validator-compatible packet closes the gap between hostname candidate generation and evidence validation.
proposed: Use the retained GitHub Actions artifact as the first externally executed packet for candidate classification.
superseded: Treating a candidate hostname or local resolver failure as deployment proof.
unresolved: Whether the candidate resolves in GitHub Actions or public DNS.
Whether it serves the research surface.
Which exact commit, if any, is deployed.

verification.runtime_claim: not executed in GitHub Actions during this run

limits: A structurally valid packet does not prove observations were honestly collected.
DNS failure is not proof of hostname nonexistence.
Public reachability is not exact-commit or Cloudflare control-plane proof.
No diagnosis, treatment, payment, or conversion claim is authorized.
